channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: ATF accused of 'circumventing' Trump order to place DEI staff on paid leave
item.description: The ATF is coming under fire for allegedly quietly changing the job title of its DEI officer to "senior executive" as President Donald Trump shutters DEI offices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JFK's grandson says there is 'nothing heroic' about Trump's declassification order
item.description: Former President John F. Kennedy's grandson is upset with President Donald Trump's order to declassify files relating to his grandfather and great-uncle's assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to survey wildfire-damaged Southern California
item.description: Trump has threatened to withhold federal disaster aid unless leaders change the state's approach to its water management.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Florida's special congressional primaries
item.description: Republicans are eager to find replacements for former GOP Reps. Matt Gaetz and Michael Waltz in Tuesday’s special congressional primaries in Florida to add some breathing room to their slender majority in the U.S. House, but control of the chamber isn’t on the line.
item.dc:creator: Robert Yoon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tenn. Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve 3rd term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump DHS finds 'mass influx' of illegal migrants at southern border, requests assistance from all 50 states
item.description: President Trump's Department of Homeland Security finds "an actual or imminent mass influx" of illegal immigrants at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defense Department pauses all social media posts pending review by incoming secretary
item.description: In the future, all social media posts from the Department of Defense will reflect an emphasis solely on "warfighting and lethality" under President Donald Trump, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Schumer calls on Trump to declassify government files on UFOs
item.description: “Now do UFOs,” Schumer wrote on social platform X in a repost of the White House’s announcement that Trump has ordered government files on John F.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth told senator he paid $50,000 to woman who accused him of 2017 sex assault
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, paid $50,000 to the woman who accused him of sexual assault in 2017, according to answers he provided to a senator during his confirmation process that The Associated Press has obtained.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawaii's Hirono only senator to vote no on Collins, continuing partisan streak at hearings
item.description: Democratic Hawaii Sen. Mazie Hirono's partisan streak at confirmation hearings continued Thursday as the lone 'no' vote in a committee hearing to recommend VA nominee Doug Collins for confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man who took a US Capitol tour with a gun in his possession is arrested, police say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who entered the U.S. Capitol with a handgun in his possession and took a tour of the building faces weapons charges, police said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Supreme Court weighs what to do about ‘dysfunctional’ Minnesota House
item.description: The high court heard oral arguments Thursday over the temporary control of the Minnesota House. “This is a highly unusual situation we find ourselves in,” Justice Anne McKeig said during early questioning of attorneys.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs and sanctions if it does not agree to end war
item.description: It is unclear how much impact economic penalties would have on the Russian government, since they already face various sanctions imposed by the previous administration.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Congressional Black Caucus blasts Trump DEI action as step backward
item.description: The Congressional Black Caucus (CBC), one of the largest in Congress, blasted President Trump on Wednesday for rolling back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5
